target.name: Liquid AI
target.founder: Ramin Hasani, Mathias Lechner, Alexander Amini, Daniela Rus

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $2.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $250M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Field AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Twelve Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $250M

data.narrative:
Liquid AI is a Series A AI & Software company currently valued at $2.4B post-money on $287M raised. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies (1 public: Palantir; 8 private), with valuations ranging from $250M to $32.0B.

Two methods triangulate the valuation. Stage-progression applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $4.7B. Peer-median analysis across the 9-company comparable set produces $2.0B, reflecting the distribution of valuations at similar stages; the comp universe includes Field AI ($2.0B, Series A), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B, Series B), and Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B, Series B), illustrating wide dispersion within the sector.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.7B to $5.4B, with a median of $3.4B. The current $2.4B post-money sits at the lower end of this band, below the median. Key uncertainties include the wide gap between stage-progression and peer-median methods—reflecting heterogeneous investor risk appetite in frontier AI—and the large variance within the peer set itself, where late-stage comparables command substantial premiums. Validation would benefit from clearer visibility into Liquid AI's revenue, user metrics, or technical defensibility relative to peers.
